title: RSS With the Grav CMS

content:
Due to a redesign of the website, the sidebar is no longer there. Instead, RSS links are on the footer. However, tag and category filtering still work.

If you did not know, I use Grav for my website. This is the first CMS that I really enjoyed using. Drupal was too much, WordPress was difficult to hack, and I did not want to bother with anything static since it seemed like too much work. Probably was not, but eh whatever.

Anyway, RSS and Atom feeds are available in the sidebar. However, maybe you want something more fine grained. Filtering by tag? By category? No problem.

This assumes you know what RSS and Atom are. At a point in the future, I might write a post on how to set up a client. Until then, check out Nick's blog post.

Filtering is very easy. Selecting a tag on the side will filter, for example filtering by the tag radare2 would be /blog/tag:radare2. Replacing tag with category would filter by categories. To get the RSS feed, replace blog with blog.rss or blog.atom, depending on what you prefer (tip: use Atom over RSS!).
